description: v0.2 Phase 1 — Foundation. Lock dev tooling, capture frontend perf baseline, and keep the backend responsive while the cron forecast fetcher runs. Covers brief stories US-001 (Dev experience baseline), US-002 (Frontend performance pass), US-003 (Backend performance & resilience). Adds ESLint + Prettier across both packages, tightens TypeScript strictness, husky + lint-staged pre-commit, Lighthouse mobile ≥ 90 baseline, vite chunk-size warning budget, worker_threads offload of the forecast fetcher, morgan request logging with requestId propagation, and verifies MongoDB indexes. Source: tasks/prd-v02-phase-foundation.md.

userStories.title: ESLint + Prettier across ui/ and backend/
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want a single lint + format setup across both packages so I get consistent feedback and the same rules apply everywhere.
acceptanceCriteria: Add ui/eslint.config.js (ESLint flat config) using eslint-plugin-vue + @typescript-eslint + @vue/eslint-config-typescript, with eslint-config-prettier last in the config chain to disable conflicting style rules
Add backend/eslint.config.js (ESLint flat config) using @typescript-eslint, with eslint-config-prettier last
Add a single root .prettierrc that both packages defer to (no per-package Prettier config that overrides it)
ui/package.json adds scripts: 'lint': 'eslint . --max-warnings 0' and 'format': 'prettier --write .'
backend/package.json adds scripts: 'lint': 'eslint . --max-warnings 0' and 'format': 'prettier --write .'
After at most one autofix pass (eslint --fix && prettier --write), `cd ui && yarn lint` exits 0
After at most one autofix pass, `cd backend && npm run lint` exits 0
`cd ui && yarn typecheck` still passes
`cd backend && npm run typecheck` still passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: brief US-001 (Dev experience baseline). PRD US-001.

userStories.title: Tighten TypeScript strictness in both packages
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want strict TypeScript so type errors surface at edit time, not in production.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/tsconfig.json (or the referenced tsconfig.app.json) has 'strict': true, 'noUncheckedIndexedAccess': true, 'noFallthroughCasesInSwitch': true
backend/tsconfig.json has the same three flags enabled
`cd ui && yarn typecheck` passes
`cd backend && npm run typecheck` passes
Any new // @ts-expect-error or `as any` cast added solely to make the build pass has a one-line comment explaining why; no silent suppressions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: brief US-001 (Dev experience baseline). PRD US-002. Depends on US-001 only insofar as both should land before US-003's pre-commit hook is added.

userStories.title: Pre-commit hook running typecheck on staged files
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want broken types caught before commit so they never reach review.
acceptanceCriteria: Introduce a minimal root package.json (private, no app code) solely to host devDependencies husky and lint-staged
`npx husky init` (or equivalent manual setup) generates .husky/pre-commit; the hook body runs `npx lint-staged`

Root package.json `lint-staged` config: for staged *.ts/*.tsx run `eslint --fix` + `prettier --write` + `tsc --noEmit` scoped to the staged files; for staged *.vue run `eslint --fix` + `prettier --write` + `vue-tsc --noEmit` scoped to the staged files; for staged *.js/*.json/*.md run `prettier --write`
A representative typo (e.g. `const x: number = 'foo'`) in a single staged file causes the pre-commit hook to fail before the commit is created
`git commit --no-verify` continues to bypass the hook (default husky behaviour preserved)
Pre-commit completes in ≤ ~5s wall-clock for a typical 1–3 file change on a warm cache
README.md adds a short 'Pre-commit hooks' section explaining what runs and how to skip in emergencies
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: brief US-001 (Dev experience baseline). PRD US-003. Depends on US-001 (lint config) and US-002 (strict tsconfig) so the staged-file checks have something real to run.

userStories.title: Structured request logging + MongoDB index verification
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want structured request logs and confirmed indexes so I can diagnose latency issues with real data.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `morgan` to backend deps; configure with a custom JSON-shaped format string emitting at minimum: requestId, method, path, status, durationMs, timestamp
Add a request-id middleware (mounted before morgan) that generates `req.requestId = crypto.randomUUID()` and exposes it on res header `x-request-id`

Create backend/src/lib/log.ts exposing `log.info`, `log.warn`, `log.error` that emit single-line JSON to stdout including `requestId` when called from request scope (use AsyncLocalStorage to carry the id) and omit it when called from non-request scope (e.g. cron/worker)
Migrate the forecast fetcher's start/finish app log calls to use the new wrapper to prove integration
Add Mongoose schema indexes in code: `forecastSnapshotSchema.index({ beachId: 1 })` and `forecastSnapshotSchema.index({ fetchedAt: -1 })`

Boot the backend, hit `db.forecastSnapshots.getIndexes()` via mongo shell (or the existing connection), confirm both indexes are present, and capture the output in docs/perf-baseline.md under a 'Backend baseline > Indexes' subsection
`cd backend && npm run typecheck` passes
`cd backend && npm run lint` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: brief US-003 (Backend performance & resilience). PRD US-008. Sequenced before the worker offload so the worker code can use the new log wrapper from day one.

userStories.title: Backend latency baseline + offload forecast fetcher to a worker
userStories.description: As a user, I want the API to stay responsive even while the forecast fetcher runs, so list and detail views never stall during a cron cycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Measure baseline p50/p95 response times for `GET /beaches` and `GET /beaches/:id/forecast` with the cron fetcher idle (≥ 50 sequential requests, document the tool used — autocannon, hey, or a small node script — in docs/perf-baseline.md)
Record idle p50/p95 for both endpoints in docs/perf-baseline.md under 'Backend baseline > Latency (idle)'
Measure the same p50/p95 during an active forecast fetch cycle (trigger via `POST /admin/fetch-forecasts` or by tightening the cron interval); record under 'Latency (during fetch, before offload)'

Implement worker_threads offload of the forecast fetcher: a single entry point `backend/src/workers/forecastFetcher.worker.ts` that owns the Mongoose connection, runs the existing fetch + score + persist pipeline, and accepts a one-shot trigger message
Both the existing cron entry point and the existing `POST /admin/fetch-forecasts` endpoint enqueue work to the worker (single code path); neither runs the fetcher inline on the HTTP main thread
If a separate child process is chosen instead of worker_threads (e.g. due to Mongoose lifecycle issues), document the trade-off in the PR description
Re-measure p50/p95 during an active fetch after the offload; record under 'Latency (during fetch, after offload)'
After-offload during-fetch p95 ≤ idle p95 × 1.10 (≤ 10% regression) for both endpoints
Manual smoke check: trigger `POST /admin/fetch-forecasts` and concurrently hit `GET /beaches` in a separate terminal — list endpoint stays responsive (sub-second responses)
Use the log wrapper from US-004 (`log.info`) inside the worker for fetch start/finish
`cd backend && npm run typecheck` passes
`cd backend && npm run lint` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: brief US-003 (Backend performance & resilience). PRD US-007. Depends on US-004 for the structured log wrapper used inside the worker.

userStories.title: Capture Lighthouse mobile baseline and lift score to ≥ 90
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a committed Lighthouse baseline and a mobile performance score of at least 90 so we have a concrete gate for v0.2.
acceptanceCriteria: Create docs/perf-baseline.md (or extend the existing file from US-004) with a 'Frontend baseline' section
Run Lighthouse mobile (default mobile preset) against `yarn build && yarn preview` three times; record the median Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, and SEO scores
Record date and commit SHA of the run, plus the device/throttling profile (e.g. 'Lighthouse default mobile preset — Moto G4 / Slow 4G')

If the initial median Performance < 90, apply the wins from docs/v0.2-design-brief.md US-002: route-level code-splitting via dynamic `import()`, lazy-load MapLibre on `/map` only (`import('maplibre-gl')` inside the route), `loading="lazy"` on beach photo `` (or equivalent on the existing image component), and font subsetting if any custom font is used
Final recorded median Lighthouse mobile Performance ≥ 90 (hard gate — phase cannot exit otherwise)
Build chunk graph (yarn build output or vite-bundle-visualizer) confirms `maplibre-gl` is in a route-specific chunk, NOT in the main entry chunk; record the route chunk name in docs/perf-baseline.md
`cd ui && yarn typecheck` passes
`cd ui && yarn lint` passes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: home loads, list scrolls smoothly, navigating to /map still mounts the map after the lazy chunk loads
userStories.notes: Source: brief US-002 (Frontend performance pass). PRD US-005. Depends on US-001/US-002 (lint + tsconfig) and US-003 (pre-commit) only insofar as those gates must already pass on main when this story lands.

userStories.title: Bundle-size baseline and perf budget in vite.config.ts
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want bundle growth to surface as a build warning so charts and other v0.2 additions don't silently bloat the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `yarn build` and record per-chunk gzipped sizes in docs/perf-baseline.md alongside the Lighthouse numbers from US-006 (a small markdown table: chunk name | raw size | gzip size)
Identify the largest chunk's gzipped size and round up to the nearest 10 KB; multiply by 1.10 (10% headroom)
Set `build.chunkSizeWarningLimit` in ui/vite.config.ts to that value (in KB, since vite expects KB)
Add a one-line comment next to the value in vite.config.ts: '// v0.2 baseline largest chunk + 10% headroom — see docs/perf-baseline.md'
`yarn build` completes without any chunk-size warnings on the current main
`cd ui && yarn typecheck` passes
`cd ui && yarn lint` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: brief US-002 (Frontend performance pass). PRD US-006. Depends on US-006 because the budget value is the largest chunk size measured at that point.

userStories.title: Document Docker + HMR developer workflow in README.md
userStories.description: As a new contributor, I want the dev workflow documented so I can boot the stack without trial and error.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md has a 'Local development' section covering: clone → `docker compose up` (or the equivalent the repo actually uses) → which ports are exposed for UI and backend → where the UI and backend live in the tree
Section explains the `usePolling: true` HMR workaround in ui/vite.config.ts and *why* (Docker bind-mount inotify limits on macOS)
Section documents how to run the cloudflared tunnel for real-device smoke checks (referenced by Phase 1 Definition of Done in docs/v0.2-plan.md line 215)
Section lists the `lint`, `typecheck`, `format`, `build` commands for both ui/ and backend/
Section references the 'Pre-commit hooks' subsection added in US-003
Manual smoke check: a contributor following only README.md from a fresh clone can reach a running UI and backend without having to read the source
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: brief US-001 (Dev experience baseline). PRD US-004. Last in the order so it documents the scripts and hooks introduced by earlier stories — avoids the README drifting as those land.
